pr.title: [Pattern] Escalation Pathways (Agent → Human Handoff) — AI — Feedback & Control
pr.body:
## Pattern candidature: Escalation Pathways (Agent → Human Handoff)

### Category
AI — Feedback & Control

### What problem does it solve?
Structured triggers for when AI hits ambiguity, conflicts, or authorization limits - pausing gracefully for human input.

AI agents are not infallible. Well-designed escalation points prevent costly errors while maintaining autonomy where AI excels.

### First seen on
- **Product:** Replit Agent
- **URL:** https://replit.com
- **First observed (approx. date):** 2024

### Other examples in the wild
1. **Enterprise AI Governance** — Checkpoint gates for sensitive actions requiring human approval before execution
2. **Customer Support Bots** — Automatic handoff to human agent when confidence is insufficient

### Why it belongs in this library
As AI agents gain more capabilities, knowing when to stop and ask for help is critical. This pattern provides a structured framework for agent-to-human handoff with three key trigger types: confidence thresholds, authorization boundaries, and conflict detection.

### What makes a good implementation
- Clear escalation thresholds (confidence < 80%, financial actions, etc.)
- Graceful pause with state preservation
- Full context presentation to human
- Seamless resume after decision
- Timeout mechanisms for abandoned escalations

### Known pitfalls
- Escalating too frequently defeats automation benefits
- Not providing enough context for human decision
- Blocking indefinitely without reminders
- Losing agent state during handoff

---

### Files included in this PR
- [x] `src/_data/patterns/escalation-pathways.json`
- [x] `src/_data/demos/escalation-pathways.html`
- [x] `src/_data/demos/escalation-pathways.json`

### Submission checklist
- [x] I searched `src/_data/patterns/` and confirmed this pattern does not already exist
- [x] I searched open PRs and issues for duplicates
- [x] My demo is interactive and self-contained (no external dependencies)
- [x] Both `fr` and `en` titles are filled in the JSON
- [x] `firstSeenOn` contains a real product URL
- [x] At least 2 examples are listed in the JSON and in this PR description
- [x] I tested the page locally at `/fr/patterns/escalation-pathways/`
- [x] The demo fits within the height I set in `demos/escalation-pathways.json`
